classes.name: Hunter Bloodbound
classes.flavorText:
BECOMING A HUNTER BLOODBOUND ... AS A LEVEL 1 CHARACTER
• Gain all the traits in the Core Hunter Bloodbound Traits table.
• Gain the Hunter Bloodbound's level 1 features, which are listed in the Hunter Bloodbound Features table.

overview.skillProficiencies: Choose 2: Grit, Blood Nature, Athletics, Intimidation, Medicine, Perception or Survival

coreTraits.name: Skill Proficiencies
coreTraits.text: Choose 2: Grit, Blood Nature, Athletics, Intimidation, Medicine, Perception or Survival

coreTraits.name: Starting Equipment
coreTraits.text: Greataxe, 2 Handaxes and Blood-drainer's Tools (unique item)

features.name: Blood Frenzy
features.text:
You can imbue yourself with a primal power called Blood Frenzy, a force that grants you extraordinary might and resilience. You can enter it as a Bonus Action if you aren't wearing Heavy armor.
You can enter your Blood Frenzy the number of times shown for your Hunter Bloodbound level in the Blood Frenzys column of the Hunter Bloodbound Features table. You regain one expended use when you finish a Short Rest, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.
While active, your Blood Frenzy follows the rules below.
Damage Resistance. You have Resistance to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage.
Blood Frenzy Damage. When you make an attack using Strength—with either a weapon or an Unarmed Strike—and deal damage to the target, you gain a bonus to the damage that increases as you gain levels as a Hunter Bloodbound, as shown in the Blood Frenzy Damage column of the Hunter Bloodbound Features table.
Strength Advantage. You have Advantage on Strength checks and Strength saving throws.
Duration. The Blood Frenzy lasts until the end of your next turn, and it ends early if you don Heavy armor or have the Incapacitated condition. If your Blood Frenzy is still active on your next turn, you can extend the Blood Frenzy for another round by doing one of the following:
• Make an attack roll against an enemy.
• Force an enemy to make a saving throw.
• Take a Bonus Action to extend your Blood Frenzy.
Each time the Blood Frenzy is extended, it lasts until the end of your next turn.

features.name: Frenzied But Sane
features.text: You can spend one Blood Frenzy to not suffer Madness from using the power of a Transformation one time.

features.name: Danger Sense
features.text: You gain an uncanny sense of when things aren't as they should be, giving you an edge when you dodge perils. You have Advantage on Dexterity saving throws unless you have the Incapacitated condition.

features.name: Reckless Attack
features.text: You can throw aside all concern for defense to attack with increased ferocity. When you make your first attack roll on your turn, you can decide to attack recklessly. Doing so gives you Advantage on attack rolls using Strength until the start of your next turn, but attack rolls against you have Advantage during that time.

features.name: Hunter Bloodbound Subclass
features.text: You gain a Hunter Bloodbound subclass of your choice. The Path of the Berserker and the Path of the Blood Drunk subclasses are detailed after this class's description. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Hunter Bloodbound levels.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.text: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat (see chapter 4) or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Hunter Bloodbound levels 8, 12, and 16.

features.name: Extra Attack
features.text: You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

features.name: Instinctive Pounce
features.text: As part of the Bonus Action you take to enter your Blood Frenzy, you can move up to half your Speed.

features.name: Brutal Strike
features.text:
If you use Reckless Attack, you can forgo any Advantage on one Strength-based attack roll of your choice on your turn. The chosen attack roll mustn't have Disadvantage. If the chosen attack roll hits, the target takes an extra 1d10 damage of the same type dealt by the weapon or Unarmed Strike, and you can cause one Brutal Strike effect of your choice.
You have the following effect options.
Forceful Blow. The target is pushed 15 feet straight away from you. You can then move up to half your Speed straight toward the target without provoking Opportunity Attacks.
Hamstring Blow. The target's Speed is reduced by 15 feet until the start of your next turn. A target can be affected by only one Hamstring Blow at a time—the most recent one.

features.name: Relentless Blood Frenzy
features.text:
Your Blood Frenzy can keep you fighting despite grievous wounds. If you drop to 0 Hit Points while your Blood Frenzy is active and don't die outright, you can make a DC 10 Constitution saving throw. If you succeed, your Hit Points instead change to a number equal to twice your Hunter Bloodbound level.
Each time you use this feature after the first, the DC increases by 5. When you finish a Short or Long Rest, the DC resets to 10.

features.name: Weapon Mastery
features.text: Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery properties of two kinds of Simple or Martial Melee weapons of your choice. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can practice weapon drills and change one of those weapon choices.

features.name: Improved Brutal Strike
features.text:
You have honed new ways to attack furiously. The following effects are now among your Brutal Strike options.
Staggering Blow. The target has Disadvantage on the next saving throw it makes, and it can't make Opportunity Attacks until the start of your next turn.
Sundering Blow. Before the start of your next turn, the next attack roll made by another creature against the target gains a +5 bonus to the roll. An attack roll can gain only one Sundering Blow bonus.

features.name: Persistent Blood Frenzy
features.text:
When you roll Initiative, you can regain all expended uses of Blood Frenzy. After you regain uses of Blood Frenzy in this way, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest.
In addition, your Blood Frenzy is so fierce that it now lasts without you needing to do anything to extend it from round to round. Your Blood Frenzy ends early if you have the Unconscious condition (not just the Incapacitated condition) or don Heavy armor.

features.name: Improved Brutal Strike
features.text: The extra damage of your Brutal Strike increases to 2d10. In addition, you can use two different Brutal Strike effects whenever you use your Brutal Strike feature.

features.name: Indomitable Might
features.text: If your total for a Strength check or Strength saving throw is less than your Strength score, you can use that score in place of the total.

features.name: Epic Boon
features.text: You gain an Epic Boon feat (see chapter 4) or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Primal Champion
features.text: You embody primal power. Your Strength and Constitution scores increase by 4, to a maximum of 25.

subclasses.name: Path of the Berserker
subclasses.description: Channel Blood Frenzy into Violent Fury. Hunter Bloodbounds who walk the Path of the Berserker direct their Blood Frenzy primarily toward violence. Their path is one of untrammeled fury, and they thrill in the chaos of battle as they allow their Blood Frenzy to seize and empower them.

features.name: Frenzy
features.text:
If you use Reckless Attack while your Blood Frenzy is active, you deal extra damage to the first target you hit on your turn with a Strength-based attack. To determine the extra damage, roll a number of d6s equal to your Blood Frenzy Damage bonus, and add them together.
The damage has the same type as the weapon or Unarmed Strike used for the attack.

features.name: Mindless Blood Frenzy
features.text: You have Immunity to the Charmed and Frightened conditions while your Blood Frenzy is active. If you're Charmed or Frightened when you enter your Blood Frenzy, the condition ends on you.

features.name: Retaliation
features.text: When you take damage from a creature that is within 5 feet of you, you can take a Reaction to make one melee attack against that creature, using a weapon or an Unarmed Strike.

features.name: Intimidating Presence
features.text:
As a Bonus Action, you can strike terror into others with your menacing presence and primal power.
When you do so, each creature of your choice in a 30-foot Emanation originating from you must make a Wisdom saving throw (DC 8 plus your Strength modifier and Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, a creature has the Frightened condition for 10 rounds. At the end of each of the Frightened creature's turns, the creature repeats the save, ending the effect on itself on a success.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest unless you expend a use of your Blood Frenzy (no action required) to restore your use of it.

subclasses.name: Path of the Blood Drunk
subclasses.description: Channel the power of the old blood.

features.name: Further Frenzied But Sane
features.text: You can spend one Blood Frenzy to not suffer Madness from using the power of a Transformation two times while its active.

features.name: Embrace The Form
features.text: At Transform level 1 you may spend one Blood Frenzy to instead decide for yourself the roll of the d20.

features.name: Absolute Harmony
features.text: You can spend one Blood Frenzy to not suffer Madness from using the power of a Transformation three times while its active.

features.name: One Form
features.text:
You gain one transformation of your choice permanently, except the Lost Transformation.
While using this transformation, or any other transformation, you no longer suffer Madness from using transformations.
If you roll for a transformation and the result is your chosen permanent transformation, you instead gain the Rare Transformation. You only gain the Rare Transformation this way if you roll the result. If another feature lets you choose a transformation instead of rolling, you cannot choose the Rare Transformation.
Rare Transformation. The Rare Transformation is not always active. You can activate it during combat by spending 1 use of Blood Frenzy. It lasts for 3 rounds before disappearing.
When activated, your body mutates into an inhuman form. Your original arms elongate and twist into monstrous limbs, while two additional arms burst from your torso, leaving you with a total of four arms. Your legs stretch and contort with violent, unnatural force. You suffer 6 Madness.
While transformed in this way, you gain the following benefits:
Otherworldly Speed. Your movement speed increases by 15 feet, and creatures cannot make Opportunity Attacks against you.
Otherworldly Power. When you take the Attack action, you can only make Unarmed Strikes, and those Unarmed Strikes can only be used to deal damage. You cannot use them to grapple or shove.
Your Unarmed Strikes use Strength for the attack roll and damage roll. On a hit, they deal 4d12 piercing or bludgeoning damage, your choice for each attack, + your Strength modifier.
When you hit a creature with one of these Unarmed Strikes, you may divide the damage equally between the target and one other creature within your reach, rounding down.
This damage cannot be increased by feats, weapon properties, class features, or other similar effects. However, if you have the Extra Attack feature, you may still make additional Unarmed Strikes as part of the same Attack action.
Otherworldly Retaliation. While this transformation is active, when a creature within your melee reach deals melee damage to you, you can use your reaction to lash out with your mutated limbs. The creature takes 1d12 piercing damage + your Strength modifier.

classes.notes: SAVING-THROW CONFLICT: The Core Hunter Bloodbound Traits CSV and the Classes Boards table list Saving Throw Proficiencies as 'Intelligence and Wisdom', but the Players Handbook Class Overview Part 1 lists Saving Throw 'STR + CON' (Primary Ability CON, HP D12). The project README resolves this in favour of the Overview + 5e basis: Bloodbound saves = STR/CON. Both values are preserved here — overview.savingThrows uses STR/CON per the README/Handbook; coreTraits retains the verbatim 'Intelligence and Wisdom' from the trait sheet. The Bloodbound PDF could not be rendered as images (pdftoppm unavailable), but its full text was read from resources/extracted/text/Bloodbound.txt. The 'Blood Frenzy Damage' column referenced by the Blood Frenzy feature is mentioned in the rules text but its per-level values were not present in any provided table/CSV (the Features CSV only includes the 'Blood Frenzy' uses column). Path of the Blood Drunk's description in the source is literally 'Text.' (a placeholder); the subtitle 'Channel the power of the old blood' is used as the description. Minor OCR artifacts in source (e.g. 'Ri e', 'pro ciency', 'su er', 'modi er', 'e ect', 'ghting', dropped 'fi/fl/ff' ligatures) were corrected to their intended words. SOURCE CONFLICT (Sanity): the standalone class board (captured above) lists Max Sanity 18 / Sanity Die D12, but the canonical Player’s Handbook Ch.2 Core Traits list Max Sanity 20 / Sanity Die 1d20 — see master.json top-level sourceConflicts; confirm with the DM.

classes.name: Hunter Warden
classes.flavorText: You read the battlefield with a commander's eye, identifying openings, weaknesses, and moments of hesitation. Through sharp orders and tactical insight, you rally your hunter band and guide them toward decisive action.

overview.skillProficiencies: Choose 2: Perception, Investigation, Animal Handling, Survival, Presence or Persuasion

coreTraits.name: Skill Proficiencies
coreTraits.text: Choose 2: Perception, Investigation, Animal Handling, Survival, Presence or Persuasion

coreTraits.name: Starting Equipment
coreTraits.text: Hunter Rifle (unique item), Longsword, Navigators Tools, Bell, Backpack and 2 Hunting Traps

features.name: Bands Directive
features.text:
You read the battlefield with a commander's eye, identifying openings, weaknesses, and moments of hesitation. Through sharp orders and tactical insight, you rally your hunter band and guide them toward decisive action.
This command is represented by your Bands Directive die, which is a d6.
Using Bands Directive. As a Bonus Action, you can choose one creature other than yourself within 60 feet of you that can see or hear you. That creature gains one of your Bands Directive dice.
A creature can have only one Bands Directive die at a time.
Once, when the creature fails a d20 Test, it can roll the Bands Directive die and add the number rolled to the d20, potentially turning the failure into a success.
The Bands Directive die is expended when it is rolled or after a long or short rest.
Number of Uses. You can grant a Bands Directive die a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier, minimum of once. You regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.
At Higher Levels. Your Bands Directive die changes when you reach certain Hunter Warden levels, as shown in the Bands Directive Die column of the Hunter Warden Features table. The die becomes a d8 at level 5, a d10 at level 10, and a d12 at level 15.

features.name: Feel Your Enemy
features.text: As a Bonus Action, you can discern certain strengths of a creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself; you can tell the creatures Challenge Rating (CR) or Level (Lvl.) Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a Short Rest or Long Rest. You can also restore a use of the feature by expending one Bands Directive die (no action required).

features.name: Tactical Mind
features.text: You have a mind for tactics on and off the battlefield. When you fail an ability check, you can expend a use tactical mind to push yourself toward success. Rather than giving a Bands Directive die to a player you can roll it yourself and add the number rolled to the ability check, potentially turning it into a success. The Bands Directive die is expended when it is rolled.

features.name: Expertise
features.text:
You gain Expertise (see the rules glossary) in two of your skill proficiencies of your choice. Performance and Persuasion are recommended if you have proficiency in them.
At Hunter Warden level 9, you gain Expertise in two more of your skill proficiencies of your choice.

features.name: Experience
features.text:
You can add half your Proficiency Bonus (round down) to any ability check you make that uses a skill proficiency you lack and that doesn't otherwise use your Proficiency Bonus.
For example, if you make a Strength (Athletics) check and lack Athletics proficiency, you can add half your Proficiency Bonus to the check.

features.name: Hunter Warden Subclass
features.text: You gain a Hunter Warden subclass of your choice. The Commander or the Warbringer subclasses are detailed after this class's description. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Hunter Warden levels.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement
features.text: You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat (see chapter 4) or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Hunter Warden levels 8, 12, and 16.

features.name: Effectiveness
features.text: You now regain all your expended uses of Bands Directive when you finish a Short or Long Rest. In addition, you now have uses of Bands Directive die equal to your Wisdom modifier x 2

features.name: Know Your Enemy
features.text:
As a Bonus Action, you can discern certain strengths and weaknesses of a creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself; you know whether that creature has any Immunities, Resistances, or Vulnerabilities, and if the creature has any, you know what they are.
Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest. You can also restore a use of the feature by expending one Bands Directive die (no action required).

features.name: Counter
features.text: You have seen it all before. You know how to disrupt mind-influencing effects. If you or a creature within 30 feet of you fails a saving throw against an effect that applies the Charmed or Frightened condition, you can take a Reaction to cause the save to be rerolled, and the new roll has Advantage.

features.name: Indomitable
features.text: If you fail a saving throw, you can reroll it with a bonus equal to your Hunter Warden level. You must use the new roll, and you can't use this feature again until you finish a Long Rest. You can use this feature twice before a Long Rest starting at level 13 and three times before a Long Rest starting at level 17.

features.name: Weapon Mastery
features.text: Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery properties of three kinds of Simple or Martial Melee weapons of your choice. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can practice weapon drills and change one of those weapon choices.

features.name: Superior Effectiveness
features.text: You now have uses of Bands Directive die equal to your Wisdom modifier x 3

features.name: Expect Your Enemy
features.text: As a Bonus Action, you can discern what a creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself indents to do on its next turn; your DM has to tell you (if able) what the creature intends to do on its turn including first movement and first action if any. If the creature intends to use an action the DM also has to tell you (if able) all available info about that specific first action. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest. You can also restore a use of the feature by expending one Bands Directive die (no action required).

features.name: Calm Band
features.text: During a short or long rest you and the other creatures that has a Bands Directive die may roll that die in addition to their Sanity die.

features.name: Superior Directive
features.text: When you roll Initiative, you regain expended uses of Bands Directive until you have two if you have fewer than that.

features.name: Epic Boon
features.text: You gain an Epic Boon feat (see chapter 4) or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

features.name: Presence of Power
features.text: You very presence can be overwhelming. You have mastered words that rings with power in your enemies ears: as an action you can expand a Bands Directive die to command a creature within 30ft of you that can hear and see you to forfeit their next turn. This can not be used on the same creature twice.

subclasses.name: Commander
subclasses.description: A Hunter Warden subclass focused on directing companions in battle — studying enemies to grant allies advantage, fueling the band with Commander's Strike, Commanding Presence, and Rally, and revealing fatal openings in foes.

features.name: Studied Enemy
features.text: You can spend an action to study your opponents and learn from each attack they make. One creature other than yourself within 60 feet of you that can see or hear you will have Advantage on their next attack roll against another creature before the start of your next turn.

features.name: Band Commander
features.text:
Primal forces now help fuel you on your journeys, granting you the following benefits.
Commander's Strike. When you take the Attack action on your turn, you can replace one of your attacks to direct one of your companions to strike.
When you do so, choose a willing creature who can see or hear you and expend one Bands Directive Die. That creature can immediately use its Reaction to make one attack with a weapon or an Unarmed Strike, adding the Bands Directive Die to the attack's damage roll on a hit.
Commanding Presence. As a Bonus Action you can spend a Bands Directive Die to give another creature within 30 feet of yourself who can see or hear you Blood Tinge.
Rally. As a Bonus Action, you can expend one Bands Directive Die to bolster the resolve of a companion.
Choose an ally of yours within 30 feet of yourself who can see or hear you. That creature gains Temporary Hit Points equal to the Bands Directive Die roll plus half your Hunter Warden level (round down).

features.name: Master the Enemy
features.text:
As an action, you can spend one Band's Directive die to study an enemy and reveal a fatal opening in its defenses.
Choose one creature other than yourself within 60 feet of you that can see or hear you. The first time that creature makes an attack roll before the start of your next turn, a roll of 14 or higher on the d20 counts as a 20.

subclasses.name: Warbringer
subclasses.description: A Hunter Warden subclass focused on aggressive battlefield tactics — turning Bands Directive dice into defensive and offensive boosts, gaining Extra Attack, and empowering allies with War Chant.

features.name: Combat Inspiration
features.text:
You can use your wit to turn the tide of battle. A creature that has a Bands Directive die from you can use it for one of the following effects.
Defense. When the creature is hit by an attack roll, that creature can use its Reaction to roll the Bands Directive die and add the number rolled to its AC against that attack, potentially causing the attack to miss.
Offense. Immediately after the creature hits a target with an attack roll, the creature can roll the Bands Directive die and add the number rolled to the attack's damage against the target.

features.name: Extra Attack
features.text: You can attack twice instead of once whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

features.name: War Chant
features.text:
When a creature that has a Band's Directive die from you takes the Attack action on its turn, it can expend that die to make one additional attack as part of the same action.
This additional attack is made in addition to any attacks granted by Extra Attack or similar features.

classes.notes: Project README states the Hunter Warden has NO tool proficiency; this is confirmed by both the Core Traits CSV ("Tool Proficiencies: None") and the Class Overview Part 2 table. Saving Throw Proficiencies CSV reads "Wisdom + Charizma" (OCR/typo for Charisma) — corrected to Wisdom + Charisma. Starting Equipment conflict: the Core Traits CSV and Classes Boards list "Hunter Rifle (unique item), Longsword, Navigators Tools, Bell, Backpack and 2 Hunting Traps" (used here); the Players Handbook Class Overview Part 1 instead lists "Tool Belt, 1 Blood vial, Hunter Rifle, 10 bullets, Longsword, Navigators Tools, Bell, Bandolier and 2 Hunting Traps" (Bandolier instead of Backpack, plus Tool Belt/Blood vial/10 bullets). Confirm with DM. Warden.pdf could not be rendered (pdftoppm unavailable in this environment), so all feature text was sourced from the pre-extracted Warden.txt; OCR ligature artifacts (fi/ff/fl dropped, e.g. 'battle eld'→'battlefield', 'pro ciency'→'proficiency', 'su er'→'suffer', 'Ri e'→'Rifle', 'e ect'→'effect', 'in uencing'→'influencing') were repaired. The Commander and Warbringer subclass intros in the source read only placeholder 'Text.' — no real flavor text was present, so descriptions were synthesized from the features. Minor source typos preserved verbatim in feature text per faithfulness (e.g. 'LEVELE 9', 'indents to do', 'You very presence', 'rings with power', 'expand a Bands Directive die'). SOURCE CONFLICT (Sanity): the standalone class board (captured above) lists Max Sanity 15 / Sanity Die D12, but the canonical Player’s Handbook Ch.2 Core Traits list Max Sanity 14 / Sanity Die 4d4 — see master.json top-level sourceConflicts; confirm with the DM.

rites.name: Detect Eldritch Presence
rites.text:
For the duration, you sense eldritch presence if any, within 30 feet of you. If you sense such a presence, you can take the Rite Action to learn its direction and approximate distance: Close, Away, or Far.
If the presence is Close and was created by a Rite, you also learn the Rite's type.

rites.name: Eldritch Eye
rites.text:
You call upon a Patron that sees through distance, flesh, and stone. Choose one creature on the same plane of existence as you. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw, modified by how well you know the target and what physical connection you have to it. The target does not know what is watching it, only that something vast and unseen has turned its attention toward it.

Your Knowledge of the Target Is ...                 Save Modifier
Secondhand (heard of the target)                    +5
Firsthand (met the target)                          +0
Extensive (know the target well)                    -5

You Have the Target's ...                           Save Modifier
Picture or other likeness                           -2
Garment or other possession                         -4
Body part, lock of hair, or bit of nail             -10

On a successful save, the target is not affected, and you can't perform this Rite on it again until after a Long Rest.
On a failed save, the Patron opens an invisible, intangible eye within 10 feet of the target. For the duration, you can see and hear through the eye as if you were there. The eye moves with the target, remaining within 10 feet of it. If a creature can see the eye, it appears as a pale luminous orb about the size of your fist.

rites.name: True Seeing
rites.text: You call upon a Patron that will show you what mortal eyes were never meant to witness. For the duration, one willing creature you touch has Truesight with a range of 120 feet. The creature also suffers 1 Madness.

rites.name: Eldritch Blast
rites.text:
You hurl a beam of crackling energy. Make a ranged spell attack against one creature or object in range.
On a hit, the target takes 1d10 Eldritch Power damage.
rites.upgrade: Whisper Upgrade. The spell creates two beams at level 5, three beams at level 11, and four beams at level 17. You can direct the beams at the same target or at different ones. Make a separate attack roll for each beam.

rites.name: Eldritch Strike
rites.special: Special Requirements: A weapon with which you have proficiency
rites.text: Guided by your Patron's will, you make one attack with the weapon used to perform this Rite. The attack uses your Rite Performing ability for the attack and damage rolls instead of using Strength or Dexterity. If the attack deals damage, it can be Eldrich Power damage or the weapon's normal damage type (your choice).
rites.upgrade: Whisper Upgrade. Whether you deal Eldrich Power damage or the weapon's normal damage type, the attack deals extra Eldrich Power damage when you reach levels 5 (1d6), 11 (2d6), and 17 (3d6).

rites.name: Eldritch Lightning
rites.text: You call upon a Patron, letting a fragment of its voice burst from your body as crackling lightning. Each creature in a 5-foot Emanation originating from you must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or take 1d6 Lightning damage. The Rite also makes a thunderous sound can be heard up to 100 feet away.
rites.upgrade: Whisper Upgrade. The damage increases by 1d6 when you reach levels 5 (2d6), 11 (3d6), and 17 (4d6).

rites.name: Eldritch Rebuke
rites.performing: Reaction, which you take in response to taking damage from a creature that you can see within 60 feet of yourself
rites.text: The creature that damaged you is momentarily surrounded by blue flames and crackling lightning. It makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking 2d10 Fire damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.
rites.upgrade: Using a Higher-Level Strain. The damage increases by 1d10 for each Strain level above 1.

rites.name: Eldritch Chain of Bolts
rites.text:
You invoke a Patron of storm and ruin, causing a beam of crackling lightning to lance toward a creature within range.
Make a ranged Rite attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes 2d12 Lightning damage. Until the Rite ends, you can take a Bonus Action on each of your turns to cause the lightning to surge through the target, dealing 1d12 Lightning damage automatically.
The Rite ends if the target is ever outside the Rite's range or has Total Cover from you.
rites.upgrade: Using Higher-Level Strain. The initial damage increases by 1d12 for each Strain level above 1.

rites.name: Darkness
rites.text: For the duration, otherworldly Darkness spreads from a point within range and fills a 15-foot-radius Sphere. Darkvision can't see through it, and light can't illuminate it.

rites.name: Minor Illusion
rites.text:
A patron will make a sound or an image of an object within range that lasts for the duration. See the descriptions below for the effects of each. The illusion ends if you perform this rite again.
If a creature takes a Study action to examine the sound or image, the creature can determine that it is an illusion with a successful Intelligence (Investigation) check against your rites save DC. If a creature discerns the illusion for what it is, the illusion becomes faint to the creature.
Sound. If you create a sound, its volume can range from a whisper to a scream. It can be your voice, someone else's voice, a roar, a beating of a heart, or any other sound you choose. The sound continues unabated throughout the duration, or you can make discrete sounds at different times before the spell ends.
Image. If you create an image of an object-such as a chair, muddy footprints, or a small chest-it must be no larger than a 5-foot Cube. The image can't create sound, light, smell, or any other sensory effect. Physical interaction with the image reveals it to be an illusion, since things can pass through it.

rites.name: Shattered Reflection
rites.text:
You call upon a Patron of fractured sight, causing three illusory duplicates of yourself to appear in your space. Until the Rite ends, the duplicates move with you and mimic your actions, shifting constantly so it is impossible to know which image is real.
Each time a creature hits you with an attack roll during the Rite's duration, roll a d6 for each duplicate you have remaining. If any of the d6s roll a 3 or higher, one duplicate is hit instead of you, and that duplicate is destroyed.
The duplicates ignore all other damage and effects. The Rite ends early when all three duplicates are destroyed.
A creature is unaffected by this Rite if it has the Blinded condition, Blindsight, or Truesight.

rites.name: Mindcrack
rites.text: You try to temporarily break the mind of one creature you can see within range, opening their minds to the words of your patron through whispers. The target must succeed on an Intelligence saving throw or take 1d6 Mind damage and subtract 1d4 from the next saving throw it makes before the end of your next turn.
rites.upgrade: Whisper Upgrade. The damage increases by 1d6 when you reach levels 5 (2d6), 11 (3d6), and 17 (4d6).

rites.name: Mindgrab
rites.text: By your words a patron will attach itself onto the mind of one creature you can see within range. The target makes a Wisdom saving throw, taking 3d8 Mind damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. On a failed save, you also always know the target's location until the spell ends, but only while the two of you are on the same plane of existence. While you have this knowledge, the target can't become hidden from you, and if it has the Invisible condition, it gains no benefit from that condition against you.
rites.upgrade: Using a Higher-Level Strain. The damage increases by 1d8 for each Strain level above 2.

rites.name: Enthrall
rites.text: You word out forbidden truths gifted by a Patron whose words mortal minds struggle to comprehend. You suffer 1 Madness. Creatures of your choice that you can see within range must make a Wisdom saving throw. A creature currently fighting you or your allies automatically succeeds on this saving throw. On a failed save, a creature becomes captivated by the impossible words and suffers a –10 penalty to Perception checks and Passive Perception until the Rite ends.

rites.name: Eldritch Cacophony
rites.text:
You invoke a Patron of forbidden knowledge, flooding the minds of nearby creatures with fragments of truths they were never meant to comprehend.
Each creature in a 20-foot-radius Sphere centered on a point within range must make an Intelligence saving throw.
On a failed save, a creature takes 8d6 Mind damage, and its thoughts become muddled for 10 rounds. While muddled, it subtracts 1d6 from Attack Rolls, Ability Checks, and Constitution saving throws made to maintain Concentration.
At the end of each of its turns, the creature may repeat the Intelligence saving throw, ending the muddled effect on itself on a success.
On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage and suffers no additional effects.
rites.upgrade: Using Higher-Level Strain. If this Rite is performed with a Strain above Level 5, the damage increases by 2d6 for each Strain level above 5.

rites.name: Mind Shatter
rites.text:
You invoke a Patron of unfathomable power, unleashing a fragment of its eldritch knowledge into the mind of a creature you can see within range.
The target must make an Intelligence saving throw. On a failed save, it takes 10d12 Mind damage. For the next 10 rounds, the target can't perform Rites, and its Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma modifiers become -5.
On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage and suffers no additional effects.

rites.name: Armor of Agathys
rites.text:
You call upon a Patron of frozen ruin, wreathing your body in otherworldly frost. You gain 5 Temporary Hit Points.
If a creature hits you with a melee attack while you have these Temporary Hit Points, that creature takes 5 Cold damage. This Rite ends early if you have no Temporary Hit Points remaining.
rites.upgrade: Using Higher-Level Strain. The Temporary Hit Points and Cold damage both increase by 5 for each Strain level above 1.

rites.name: Foresight
rites.text:
You call upon a Patron that peers beyond the veil of time and fate. By its guidance, a willing creature you touch is granted fleeting glimpses of the immediate future, allowing it to perceive danger moments before it occurs.
For the duration, the target has Advantage on all D20 Tests, and other creatures have Disadvantage on attack rolls against it.
This Rite ends early if you perform it again.

rites.name: Third Hand
rites.text:
A spectral, floating hand not from this world appears at a point you choose within range. The hand lasts for the duration. The hand vanishes if it is ever more than 30 feet away from you or if you perform this rite again. When you perform the rite, you can use the hand to manipulate an object, open an unlocked door or container, stow or retrieve an item from an open container, or pour the contents out of a vial.
As a Rite action on your later turns, you can control the hand thus again. As part of that action, you can move the hand up to 30 feet.
The hand can't attack, or carry more than 10 pounds.

rites.name: Arms of Hadar
rites.text:
You invoke Hadar, the Dark Hunger Between the Stars, causing writhing tendrils to erupt from your body and lash out at nearby creatures.
Each creature in a 10-foot Emanation originating from you must make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 2d6 Acid damage and can't take Reactions until the start of its next turn. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage only.
rites.upgrade: Using Higher-Level Strain. The damage increases by 1d6 for each Strain level above 1.

rites.name: Hadar's Grasp
rites.text:
You invoke Hadar, the Dark Hunger Between the Stars, causing a massive black tendril to erupt from the ground beneath a creature you can see within range.
The target must make a Strength saving throw.
On a failed save, the target takes 2d6 Bludgeoning damage and gains the Restrained condition. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage and suffers no additional effects.
At the end of each of its turns, a Restrained creature can repeat the saving throw, ending the condition on itself on a success.
The tendril remains in its space for the duration. If the tendril is not currently Restraining a creature, a creature that enters a space within 5 feet of it for the first time on a turn, or starts its turn there, must make the saving throw against this Rite. On a failed save, the creature takes 2d6 Bludgeoning damage and becomes Restrained by the tendril.
If a creature dies while Restrained by the tendril, Hadar claims its victim. The creature and everything it carries vanish into the void between the stars, and the Rite immediately ends.
rites.upgrade: Using Higher-Level Strain. The damage increases by 1d6 for each Strain level above 2.

rites.name: Unknown Realm
rites.text: By your words an unknown realm will appear in a 30-foot Cube within range. The realm appears for a moment and then vanishes. Each creature in the area who can see the realm must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or suffer 2 Madness, have the Incapacitated condition and a Speed of 0. The condition will remain for the duration or end for an affected creature if it takes any damage or if someone else uses an action to shake the creature out of its stupor.

rites.name: Hadar's Black Tentacles
rites.text:
You invoke Hadar, the Dark Hunger Between the Stars, tearing open a brief passage for its writhing limbs to enter the world.
Squirming black tentacles fill a 20-foot square on ground you can see within range. For the duration, the area is Difficult Terrain.
Each creature in the area must make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 3d6 Bludgeoning damage and has the Restrained condition until the Rite ends. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage and isn't Restrained.
A creature must also make this save when it enters the area for the first time on a turn or ends its turn there. A creature makes this save only once per turn.
A Restrained creature can take an Action to make a Strength (Athletics) check against your Rite save DC, ending the condition on itself on a success.

rites.name: Call Lesser Starborn
rites.text:
You invoke a Patron from beyond the stars, tearing open a fleeting breach in reality and calling forth one of its lesser servants.
A Lesser Starborn manifests in an unoccupied space you can see within range. The creature uses the Lesser Starborn stat block. The Lesser Starborn disappears when it drops to 0 Hit Points or when this Rite ends. The Lesser Starborn is friendly to you and your allies. In combat, it shares your Initiative count but takes its turn immediately after yours. The Lesser Starborn obeys your verbal commands (no Action required by you). If you issue no commands, it takes the Dodge Action and uses its movement to avoid danger.
A Lesser Starborn is an unnatural creature of the outer dark. Its form varies according to the Patron that answered your call. Some resemble eyeless masses of flesh and tendrils, while others appear as twisted creatures stitched together from impossible anatomy and starlit void.
rites.upgrade: Using Higher-Level Strain. The Lesser Starborn gains 10 Temporary Hit Points for each Strain level above 5.

rites.name: Call Starborn Horror
rites.text:
You tear open reality and call forth a true Starborn Horror, a servant of the outer dark that was never meant to enter the mortal world.
When the Starborn Horror manifests, each creature within a 30-foot-radius Sphere centered on a point within range must make a Wisdom saving throw.
On a failed save, a creature takes 10d10 Mind damage, suffers 6 Madness, and gains the Frightened condition for the duration.
On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage and suffers 2 Madness.
Each other creature that can see or hear the Starborn Horror suffers 2 Madness.
You suffer 6 Madness when you perform this Rite.

The Starborn Horror uses the Starborn Horror stat block. It is not friendly to you or your allies, nor is it inherently hostile to your enemies. The Horror acts according to its own unknowable will.
At the start of each of its turns, roll on the Starborn Horror Behavior Table to determine which action it takes. When determining targets, use your Madness die system.
The Starborn Horror disappears when the Rite ends.

rites.name: Misty Step
rites.text: Briefly surrounded by silvery mist, you teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see.

rites.name: Plane Shift
rites.text: You and up to eight willing creatures who link hands in a circle are transported to a different plane of existence.

rites.name: Eldritch Rebuke
rites.performing: Reaction, which you take in response to taking damage from a creature that you can see within 60 feet of yourself
rites.text: The creature that damaged you is momentarily surrounded by blue flames and crackling lightning. It makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking 2d10 Fire damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.
rites.upgrade: Using a Higher-Level Strain. The damage increases by 1d10 for each Strain level above 1.

rites.name: Eldritch Chain of Bolts
rites.text: You invoke a Patron of storm and ruin, causing a beam of crackling lightning to lance toward a creature within range. Make a ranged Rite attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes 2d12 Lightning damage. Until the Rite ends, you can take a Bonus Action on each of your turns to cause the lightning to surge through the target, dealing 1d12 Lightning damage automatically. The Rite ends if the target is ever outside the Rite's range or has Total Cover from you.
rites.upgrade: Using Higher-Level Strain. The initial damage increases by 1d12 for each Strain level above 1.

rites.name: Darkness
rites.text: For the duration, otherworldly Darkness spreads from a point within range and fills a 15-foot-radius Sphere. Darkvision can't see through it, and light can't illuminate it.

rites.name: Armor of the Drowned Star
rites.text: You call upon a Patron of frozen ruin, wreathing your body in otherworldly frost. You gain 5 Temporary Hit Points. If a creature hits you with a melee attack while you have these Temporary Hit Points, that creature takes 5 Cold damage. This Rite ends early if you have no Temporary Hit Points remaining.
rites.upgrade: Using Higher-Level Strain. The Temporary Hit Points and Cold damage both increase by 5 for each Strain level above 1.

rites.name: Shattered Reflection
rites.text: You call upon a Patron of fractured sight, causing three illusory duplicates of yourself to appear in your space. Until the Rite ends, the duplicates move with you and mimic your actions, shifting constantly so it is impossible to know which image is real. Each time a creature hits you with an attack roll during the Rite's duration, roll a d6 for each duplicate you have remaining. If any of the d6s roll a 3 or higher, one duplicate is hit instead of you, and that duplicate is destroyed. The duplicates ignore all other damage and effects. The Rite ends early when all three duplicates are destroyed. A creature is unaffected by this Rite if it has the Blinded condition, Blindsight, or Truesight.

rites.name: Misty Step
rites.text: Briefly surrounded by silvery mist, you teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. You try not to fathom where you just passed through, suffer 3 Madness.

rites.name: Mindgrab
rites.text: By your words a patron will attach itself onto the mind of one creature you can see within range. The target makes a Wisdom saving throw, taking 3d8 Mind damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. On a failed save, you also always know the target's location until the spell ends, but only while the two of you are on the same plane of existence. While you have this knowledge, the target can't become hidden from you, and if it has the Invisible condition, it gains no benefit from that condition against you.
rites.upgrade: Using a Higher-Level Strain. The damage increases by 1d8 for each Strain level above 2.

rites.name: Enthrall
rites.text: You word out forbidden truths gifted by a Patron whose words mortal minds struggle to comprehend. You suffer 2 Madness. Creatures of your choice that you can see within range must make a Wisdom saving throw. A creature currently fighting you or your allies automatically succeeds on this saving throw. On a failed save, a creature becomes captivated by the impossible words and suffers a -10 penalty to Perception checks and Passive Perception until the Rite ends.

rites.name: Eldritch Cacophony
rites.text: You invoke a Patron of forbidden knowledge, flooding the minds of nearby creatures with fragments of truths they were never meant to comprehend. Each creature in a 20-foot-radius Sphere centered on a point within range must make an Intelligence saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 8d6 Mind damage, and its thoughts become muddled for 10 rounds. While muddled, it subtracts 1d6 from Attack Rolls, Ability Checks, and Constitution saving throws made to maintain Concentration. At the end of each of its turns, the creature may repeat the Intelligence saving throw, ending the muddled effect on itself on a success. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage and suffers no additional effects.
rites.upgrade: Using Higher-Level Strain. If this Rite is performed with a Strain above Level 5, the damage increases by 2d6 for each Strain level above 5.

rites.name: Detect Eldritch Presence
rites.text: For the duration, you sense eldritch presence if any, within 30 feet of you. If you sense such a presence, you can take the Rite Action to learn its direction and approximate distance: Close, Away, or Far. If the presence is Close and was created by a Rite, you also learn the Rite's type.

rites.name: Eldritch Eye
rites.special: Save modifiers — Your Knowledge of the Target Is...: Secondhand (heard of the target) +5; Firsthand (met the target) +0; Extensive (know the target well) -5. You Have the Target's...: Picture or other likeness -2; Garment or other possession -4; Body part, lock of hair, or bit of nail -10.
rites.text: You call upon a Patron that sees through distance, flesh, and stone. Choose one creature on the same plane of existence as you. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw, modified by how well you know the target and what physical connection you have to it. The target does not know what is watching it, only that something vast and unseen has turned its attention toward it. On a successful save, the target is not affected, and you can't perform this Rite on it again until after a Long Rest. On a failed save, the Patron opens an invisible, intangible eye within 10 feet of the target. For the duration, you can see and hear through the eye as if you were there. The eye moves with the target, remaining within 10 feet of it. If a creature can see the eye, it appears as a pale luminous orb about the size of your fist.

rites.name: Arms of Hastur
rites.text: You invoke Yog-Sothoth, the Dark Hunger Between the Stars, causing writhing tendrils to erupt from your body and lash out at nearby creatures. Each creature in a 10-foot Emanation originating from you must make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 2d6 Acid damage and can't take Reactions until the start of its next turn. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage only.
rites.upgrade: Using Higher-Level Strain. The damage increases by 1d6 for each Strain level above 1.

rites.name: Grasp of Yog-Sothoth
rites.text: You invoke Yog-Sothoth, the Dark Hunger Between the Stars, causing a massive black tendril to erupt from the ground beneath a creature you can see within range. The target must make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes 2d6 Bludgeoning damage and gains the Restrained condition. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage and suffers no additional effects. At the end of each of its turns, a Restrained creature can repeat the saving throw, ending the condition on itself on a success. The tendril remains in its space for the duration. If the tendril is not currently Restraining a creature, a creature that enters a space within 5 feet of it for the first time on a turn, or starts its turn there, must make the saving throw against this Rite. On a failed save, the creature takes 2d6 Bludgeoning damage and becomes Restrained by the tendril. If a creature dies while Restrained by the tendril, Yog-Sothoth claims its victim. The creature and everything it carries vanish into the void between the stars, and the Rite immediately ends.
rites.upgrade: Using Higher-Level Strain. The damage increases by 1d6 for each Strain level above 2.

rites.name: Unknown Realm
rites.text: By your words an unknown realm will appear in a 30-foot Cube within range. The realm appears for a moment and then vanishes. Each creature in the area who can see the realm must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or suffer 2 Madness, have the Incapacitated condition and a Speed of 0. The condition will remain for the duration or end for an affected creature if it takes any damage or if someone else uses an action to shake the creature out of its stupor.

rites.name: Grasp of the Starless Deep
rites.text: You invoke something vast and nameless beyond the veil, tearing open a brief wound in the world through which writhing limbs of the outer dark reach forth. Squirming black tentacles fill a 20-foot square on ground you can see within range. For the duration, the area is Difficult Terrain. Each creature in the area must make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 3d6 Bludgeoning damage and has the Restrained condition until the Rite ends. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage and isn't Restrained. A creature must also make this save when it enters the area for the first time on a turn or ends its turn there. A creature makes this save only once per turn. A Restrained creature can take an Action to make a Strength (Athletics) check against your Rite save DC, ending the condition on itself on a success.

rites.name: Call Lesser Starborn
rites.special: Hidden Truths. Uses the Lesser Starborn stat block.
rites.text: You invoke a Patron from beyond the stars, tearing open a fleeting breach in reality and calling forth one of its lesser servants. A Lesser Starborn manifests in an unoccupied space you can see within range. The creature uses the Lesser Starborn stat block. The Lesser Starborn disappears when it drops to 0 Hit Points or when this Rite ends. The Lesser Starborn is friendly to you and your allies. In combat, it shares your Initiative count but takes its turn immediately after yours. The Lesser Starborn obeys your verbal commands (no Action required by you). If you issue no commands, it takes the Dodge Action and uses its movement to avoid danger. A Lesser Starborn is an unnatural creature of the outer dark. Its form varies according to the Patron that answered your call. Some resemble eyeless masses of flesh and tendrils, while others appear as twisted creatures stitched together from impossible anatomy and starlit void.
rites.upgrade: Using Higher-Level Strain. The Lesser Starborn gains 10 Temporary Hit Points for each Strain level above 5.

rites.name: True Seeing
rites.text: You call upon a Patron that will show you what mortal eyes were never meant to witness. For the duration, one willing creature you touch has Truesight with a range of 120 feet. The creature also suffers 4 Madness.

rites.name: Plane Shift
rites.text: You and up to eight willing creatures who link hands in a circle are transported to a different plane of existence...

rites.name: Mind Shatter
rites.text: You invoke a Patron of unfathomable power, suffer 3 Madness, unleashing a fragment of its eldritch knowledge into the mind of a creature you can see within range. The target must make an Intelligence saving throw. On a failed save, it takes 10d12 Mind damage. For the next 10 rounds, the target can't perform Rites, and its Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma modifiers become -5. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage and suffers no additional effects.

rites.name: Foresight
rites.text: You call upon a Patron that peers beyond the veil of time and fate. By its guidance, a willing creature you touch is granted fleeting glimpses of the immediate future, allowing it to perceive danger moments before it occurs. For the duration, the target has Advantage on all D20 Tests, and other creatures have Disadvantage on attack rolls against it. This Rite ends early if you perform it again.

rites.name: Call Starborn Horror
rites.special: Hidden Truths. Uses the Starborn Horror stat block; roll on the Starborn Horror Behavior Table each of its turns.
rites.text: You tear open reality and call forth a true Starborn Horror, a servant of the outer dark that was never meant to enter the mortal world. When the Starborn Horror manifests, each creature within a 30-foot-radius Sphere centered on a point within range must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 10d10 Mind damage, suffers 10 Madness, and gains the Frightened condition for the duration. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage and suffers 5 Madness. Each other creature that can see or hear the Starborn Horror suffers 4 Madness. You suffer 10 Madness when you perform this Rite. The Starborn Horror uses the Starborn Horror stat block. It is not friendly to you or your allies, nor is it inherently hostile to your enemies. The Horror acts according to its own unknowable will. At the start of each of its turns, roll on the Starborn Horror Behavior Table to determine which action it takes. When determining targets, use your Madness die system. The Starborn Horror disappears when the Rite ends.

bookOfDeepcaller.notes: Source PDF (12 pages) has an embedded text layer that pdftotext extracted cleanly, EXCEPT the font's ligatures (fl, fi, ff, ffi) which were dropped from words and dumped as gibberish strings at the end of each text block (e.g. 'if lf', 'ff ff ff lf'). These were reconstructed in context (e.g. 'ames'->'flames', 'su er'->'suffer', 'e ects'->'effects', 'modi er'->'modifier', 'rst'->'first', 'Di cult'->'Difficult', 'st'->'fist', 'esh'->'flesh', 'ooding'->'flooding', 'eeting'->'fleeting', 'ghting'->'fighting', 'bene t'->'benefit'); the reconstructed words are high-confidence. The '�10' character in Enthrall was a minus sign rendered as '-10'. No introductory/rules prose was found in the text layer — any cover/title page appears to be image-only (no extractable text); intro left empty. A 'Hidden Truths' section header precedes the higher-level rites (levels 6-9). PLANE SHIFT (Level 7) is genuinely truncated in the source with a trailing '...' ('transported to a different plane of existence...') — transcribed verbatim as found. Rites are grouped by school across pages (Evocation, Protection/Illusion/Traversal, Mind Influence, Detection, Summoning); level ordering within the document is not strictly sequential.

whispers.name: Eldritch Blast
whispers.text: You hurl a beam of crackling energy. Make a ranged spell attack against one creature or object in range. On a hit, the target takes 1d10 Eldritch Power damage.
whispers.upgrade: Whisper Upgrade. The spell creates two beams at level 5, three beams at level 11, and four beams at level 17. You can direct the beams at the same target or at different ones. Make a separate attack roll for each beam.

whispers.name: Eldritch Lightning
whispers.text: You call upon a Patron, letting a fragment of its voice burst from your body as crackling lightning. Each creature in a 5-foot Emanation originating from you must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or take 1d6 Lightning damage. The Rite also makes a thunderous sound can be heard up to 100 feet away.
whispers.upgrade: Whisper Upgrade. The damage increases by 1d6 when you reach levels 5 (2d6), 11 (3d6), and 17 (4d6).

whispers.name: Eldritch Strike
whispers.special: Special Requirements: A weapon with which you have proficiency
whispers.text: Guided by your Patron's will, you make one attack with the weapon used to perform this Rite. The attack uses your Rite Performing ability for the attack and damage rolls instead of using Strength or Dexterity. If the attack deals damage, it can be Eldrich Power damage or the weapon's normal damage type (your choice).
whispers.upgrade: Whisper Upgrade. Whether you deal Eldrich Power damage or the weapon's normal damage type, the attack deals extra Eldrich Power damage when you reach levels 5 (1d6), 11 (2d6), and 17 (3d6).

whispers.name: Mindcrack
whispers.text: You try to temporarily break the mind of one creature you can see within range, opening their minds to the words of your patron through whispers. The target must succeed on an Intelligence saving throw or take 1d6 Mind damage and subtract 1d4 from the next saving throw it makes before the end of your next turn.
whispers.upgrade: Whisper Upgrade. The damage increases by 1d6 when you reach levels 5 (2d6), 11 (3d6), and 17 (4d6).

whispers.name: Minor Illusion
whispers.text: A patron will make a sound or an image of an object within range that lasts for the duration. See the descriptions below for the effects of each. The illusion ends if you perform this rite again. If a creature takes a Study action to examine the sound or image, the creature can determine that it is an illusion with a successful Intelligence (Investigation) check against your rites save DC. If a creature discerns the illusion for what it is, the illusion becomes faint to the creature. Sound. If you create a sound, its volume can range from a whisper to a scream. It can be your voice, someone else's voice, a roar, a beating of a heart, or any other sound you choose. The sound continues unabated throughout the duration, or you can make discrete sounds at different times before the spell ends. Image. If you create an image of an object-such as a chair, muddy footprints, or a small chest-it must be no larger than a 5-foot Cube. The image can't create sound, light, smell, or any other sensory effect. Physical interaction with the image reveals it to be an illusion, since things can pass through it.

whispers.name: Third Hand
whispers.text: A spectral, floating hand not from this world appears at a point you choose within range. The hand lasts for the duration. The hand vanishes if it is ever more than 30 feet away from you or if you perform this rite again. When you perform the rite, you can use the hand to manipulate an object, open an unlocked door or container, stow or retrieve an item from an open container, or pour the contents out of a vial. As a Rite action on your later turns, you can control the hand thus again. As part of that action, you can move the hand up to 30 feet. The hand can't attack, or carry more than 10 pounds.

whispers.notes: PDF is 2 pages, image/vector text (no embedded text layer issues other than ligature loss). Extracted via pdftotext; ligature glyphs (fi/fl/ff/ffi) were dropped by the extractor and have been restored: 'different', 'proficiency', 'Influence', 'floating', 'effects'/'effect'. The source spells the damage type 'Eldritch' in Eldritch Blast but 'Eldrich' (no 't') in Eldritch Strike — preserved verbatim as written; likely a source typo. The Eldritch Lightning sentence 'makes a thunderous sound can be heard' is missing a word (e.g. 'that') in the source and is preserved verbatim. No intro/rules prose appears on the pages — content begins directly with the whisper entries. No unreadable or illegible regions.
